articles.title: Solid v2.0.0 Beta: The <Suspense> is Over
articles.description: After a long experimental phase, Solid 2.0’s first beta lands with first-class async support where computations can return Promises or async iterables, and the reactive graph suspends and resumes around them natively. <Suspense> is retired in favor of <Loading> for initial renders, and mutations get a first-class action() primitive with optimistic support. For existing users the breaking changes are substantial, but there’s a migration guide.
articles.author: Ryan Carniato

articles.title: TypeScript 6.0 Release Candidate
articles.description: v6.0 is primarily a stepping stone to the eventual Go-powered native TypeScript 7.0 due later this year and all the necessary tsconfig.json changes will put you in a good position for the future. There are only a few small changes in the RC vs the recent beta.
articles.author: Daniel Rosenwasser (Microsoft)

articles.title: Seven Years to TypeScript: Migrating 11,000 Files at Patreon
articles.description: The popular creator platform had a million lines of JavaScript on its hands, and while adopting TypeScript on new code was going well, converting all their code was a daunting task. This retrospective covers the tools and techniques involved.
articles.author: Gavy Aggarwal (Patreon)

articles.title: Building a Real-Time Todo App with Jazz and Vue 3
articles.description: Jazz is a distributed database with real-time syncing and local reactive JSON state.
articles.author: Alexander Opalic

articles.title: A Tale of Stealing npm Publish Tokens by Opening a GitHub Issue
articles.description: A repo had an AI-powered issue triage system that ran when any issue was opened, and the issue’s title was fed directly into the prompt… and that was just step one of the three-stage attack explained here.
articles.author: Neciu Dan

articles.title: Wikipedia Hit by Self-Propagating JavaScript Worm
articles.description: A writeup of how a dormant script, accidentally triggered by a Wikimedia employee, exploited a shared global script and vandalized nearly 4,000 pages on Wikipedia’s Meta-Wiki.
articles.author: Lawrence Abrams (Bleeping Computer)

articles.title: RevoGrid: High-Performance Data Grid Component
articles.description: A high-performance data grid control built for massive datasets. Integrates with Vue, Angular, React, Svelte, or can be used from vanilla JS. Live demo and GitHub repo.
articles.author: Revolist OU

articles.title: ArkType 2.2: Use Your TypeScript Types as Runtime Validators
articles.description: A TypeScript-first validation library where types and validators are the same thing. Write a type once and it becomes both the static type and the runtime validator. In v2.2, type.fn brings runtime-validated functions, checking inputs and return values automatically.
articles.author: ArkType

articles.title: TinyBase 8.0: A Reactive Data Store for Local-First Apps
articles.description: A reactive data store and sync engine that can be used as the entire backend for many types of app. It can stand alone or integrate with all sorts of things like cloud storage, client-side stores, SQL databases, etc. v8.0 adds a middleware feature and the ability to store objects and arrays in its ‘cells’.
articles.author: James Pearce

articles.title: 📄 VMPrint: Pure JS Typesetting Engine for Perfect PDF Output
articles.description: It’s common to defer to headless Chrome instances for print-to-PDF type work, but VMPrint “guarantees identical layout given identical input, down to the sub-point position of every glyph.” Here’s a sample PDF.
articles.author: Cosmic Iron

articles.description: TC39's 113th meeting has just started with the topic of taking the Temporal API to stage 4 high on the agenda.
📊 Minification Benchmarks is a frequently updated comparison of different JavaScript minifiers. SWC is in the lead overall, but Minify and Oxc's minifier aren't far behind and are much faster.
11ty (Eleventy) started life as a Node.js-powered static site generator, but now it's becoming Build Awesome, a project under the Font Awesome umbrella.
🇫🇷 The dotJS conference is back in Paris this September 1. Its CFP is open until April 28.
Node.js 25.8.0 (Current) – Adds a --permission-audit option to run Node's permission model in a warning-only mode.
Astro 6.0 – astro dev is now using Vite’s new Environment API so you can now run your exact prod runtime during dev. Also, a new Fonts API takes care of custom fonts.
Knockout 3.5.2 – The classic MVVM library gets its first update in six years!
ESLint v10.0.3 and ESLint v9.39.4
Ember 6.11, Ionic Framework 8.8, React Native 0.85 RC.0, pnpm v10.32.0, Recharts 3.8, Jest 30.3

articles.title: Using CSS Animations as State Machines
articles.description: Patrick, of Microsoft’s Edge team, was inspired by the new focusgroup feature to come up with a way to style an element depending on whether it had ever been focused before using only CSS. And he had a brainwave…
articles.author: Patrick Brosset

articles.title: Request for Developer Feedback: focusgroup
articles.description: This proposed HTML attribute aims to provide a declarative way to add keyboard arrow-key navigation to composite widgets (such as toolbars, tablists, menus, etc) replacing “hundreds of lines of boilerplate”.
articles.author: Jacques Newman (Chrome Developers)

articles.title: Eleventy is now Build Awesome
articles.description: Popular static-site generator Eleventy (a.k.a. 11ty) is now Build Awesome, bringing it in line with the same naming convention of both Font Awesome and Web Awesome. A Kickstarter for the Pro version of the project (which adds a visual editor) launched alongside this rebrand, but has since been paused.
articles.author: Zach Leatherman

articles.title: Building a New Flash
articles.description: Newgrounds was essentially the de facto home of Flash-based content on the web 20 years ago. Steve Jobs’ thoughts on the tech almost ended the medium, but now a Newgrounds veteran is looking to reimagine Flash for the modern day: “I’m making Flash if Flash was built in 2026”.
articles.author: Bill Premo

articles.title: The Value of z-index
articles.description: Amit points out that, with far too many projects, z-index values often become a mess of random, arbitrary high numbers just as a means to avoid UI elements being hidden. He highlights the issues with such an approach, and a solution: tokenization of z-index values.
articles.author: Amit Sheen

articles.title: ⚓️ The Big Gotcha of Anchor Positioning
articles.description: There’s a video here where Chris quickly demonstrates the “weird new problem that CSS has given us”. One that’s worth being aware of, I reckon, as it could cause headaches.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: How Fast is Fast Enough? Rethinking Web Performance in Pragmagical Terms
articles.description: What does it take to get ‘fast enough’ web performance and what does that mean? Tammy moves beyond simple metrics to embrace a more practical, user-centric approach.
articles.author: SpeedCurve

articles.title: Building Async Page Transitions in Vanilla JavaScript
articles.description: A look at how to approach building a lightweight SPA router with true async crossfade page transitions using Vanilla JavaScript, GSAP, and Vite.
articles.author: Valentin Mor

articles.title: The Enforced Accessibility of the Geolocation Element
articles.description: “I’m not sure if we’ve had an element in HTML that behaves quite like this before.”
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: One CSS Property That Makes Numbers Look Instantly Better
articles.description: A quick tip for using tabular (as opposed to proportional) spacing via tabular-nums. Particularly nice when used on numerical data.
articles.author: Amit Merchant

articles.title: Native Random Values in CSS
articles.description: Browser support is limited so far, but this is a good review of the future potential of random() and random-item().
articles.author: Alvaro Montoro

articles.title: Reveal.js 6.0: The HTML Presentation Framework
articles.description: A long-standing way to bring elegant presentations to anyone with a browser. v6.0 has some breaking changes, switches to Vite, and introduces an official React wrapper, but you can still write presentations just using HTML as always.
articles.author: Hakim El Hattab

articles.title: Inspira UI: Open Source Components to Build Animated Interfaces with Vue & Nuxt
articles.description: Lots of neat examples to try out in the growing list of components, all of which can be customized with styling done via Tailwind.
articles.author: Rahul Vashishtha

articles.title: Photo Palettes: A Tool to Generate Color Palettes from Photos
articles.description: Upload a photo and the tool will generate a palette, which you can customize live directly on the pixels of the photo. Includes predefined palettes and also available as a mobile app and Figma plugin.
articles.author: Photo Palettes

articles.title: All SVG Icons: Browse and Download 250,000+ Free SVG Icons From 200+ Curated Icon Libraries
articles.description: This might be one of the largest single-source icon directories, and it’s nicely designed and easy to use — there's a lot here. You can customize and preview any icon, download in different formats, and copy the code for SVG, React, Vue, Svelte, etc.
articles.author: Amit Yadav

articles.title: 📊 A Suite of JavaScript Minification Benchmarks
articles.description: A frequently updated benchmark suite and results comparing the speed and quality of JavaScript minification across tools including esbuild, Bun, SWC, and Uglify.
articles.author: Hiroki Osame
